questions.q: In which 1960 historical epic did Madhubala play the legendary courtesan Anarkali?
questions.explanation: Madhubala played Anarkali in K. Asif's 1960 epic Mughal-e-Azam, one of Indian cinema's most enduring roles.

questions.q: In which industry was the actress Madhubala primarily a star?
questions.explanation: Madhubala was a leading actress of Hindi cinema, with her career peaking in the 1950s.

questions.q: In which year was the actress Madhubala born?
questions.explanation: Madhubala was born on 14 February 1933 in Delhi.

questions.q: At what age did the actress Madhubala die in 1969?
questions.explanation: Madhubala died on 23 February 1969 at the age of 36, just days after her 36th birthday.

questions.q: Which 1949 mystery film, featuring the song 'Aayega Aanewala', established Madhubala as a star?
questions.explanation: Kamal Amrohi's 1949 gothic mystery Mahal was Madhubala's breakthrough as a leading lady.

questions.q: What was the underlying cause of Madhubala's death in 1969?
questions.explanation: Madhubala suffered from a congenital heart defect (a hole in the heart) that led to her early death at 36.

questions.q: In the 1955 comedy Mr. & Mrs. '55, Madhubala starred opposite which actor-director?
questions.explanation: Madhubala starred opposite Guru Dutt, who also directed, in the 1955 romantic comedy Mr. & Mrs. '55.

questions.q: Which playback singer sang 'Aayega Aanewala', the haunting song picturised on Madhubala in the 1949 film Mahal?
questions.explanation: 'Aayega Aanewala' from Mahal (1949) was sung by Lata Mangeshkar and became one of the songs that established her as a leading playback voice.

questions.q: In which city was the actress Madhubala born?
questions.explanation: Madhubala was born in Delhi in 1933 and her family later moved to Bombay.

questions.q: Madhubala's career as a leading actress reached its peak during which decade?
questions.explanation: Madhubala's career peaked in the 1950s with hits such as Mahal, Mr. & Mrs. '55 and Howrah Bridge.

questions.q: What was Madhubala's birth name before she adopted her screen name?
questions.explanation: Madhubala was born Mumtaz Jehan Begum Dehlavi and was renamed Madhubala in films.

questions.q: Which playback singer recorded the flirtatious number 'Aaiye Meherbaan', picturised on Madhubala in the 1958 film Howrah Bridge?
questions.explanation: 'Aaiye Meherbaan' in Howrah Bridge (1958), composed by O. P. Nayyar, was sung by Asha Bhosle and picturised on Madhubala.

questions.q: Which singer-actor did Madhubala marry in 1960?
questions.explanation: Madhubala married singer and actor Kishore Kumar in 1960; they had earlier co-starred in films like Chalti Ka Naam Gaadi.

questions.q: In which 1958 comedy did Madhubala star alongside the three brothers Ashok Kumar, Anoop Kumar and Kishore Kumar?
questions.explanation: Madhubala starred in the 1958 comedy Chalti Ka Naam Gaadi opposite Kishore Kumar and his brothers Ashok Kumar and Anoop Kumar.

questions.q: In the 1958 thriller Howrah Bridge, Madhubala was paired with which leading man?
questions.explanation: Madhubala starred opposite Ashok Kumar in the 1958 crime thriller Howrah Bridge.

questions.q: Which famous song picturised on Madhubala in Mughal-e-Azam features the defiant line 'Pyar kiya to darna kya'?
options: Jab Pyar Kiya To Darna Kya
questions.explanation: 'Jab Pyar Kiya To Darna Kya', sung by Lata Mangeshkar, was picturised on Madhubala in the Sheesh Mahal sequence of Mughal-e-Azam.

questions.q: Which actor played the prince Salim opposite Madhubala's Anarkali in Mughal-e-Azam (1960)?
questions.explanation: Dilip Kumar played Prince Salim opposite Madhubala's Anarkali in Mughal-e-Azam.

questions.q: Madhubala made her film debut as a child actress in the 1942 film Basant under which screen name?
questions.explanation: Madhubala began acting as a child under the name Baby Mumtaz, with Basant (1942) among her early films.

questions.q: Which 1954 social drama, directed by Mehboob Khan, paired Madhubala with Dilip Kumar?
questions.explanation: Mehboob Khan's Amar (1954) paired Madhubala with Dilip Kumar; the two also worked together in Tarana (1951).

questions.q: Mughal-e-Azam, in which Madhubala starred, was directed by which filmmaker?
questions.explanation: Mughal-e-Azam (1960) was directed by K. Asif over many years of production.

questions.q: Because of her beauty and charm, Madhubala was popularly nicknamed in the press as which of the following?
questions.explanation: Madhubala was widely called 'The Venus of Indian cinema' for her beauty and screen presence.

questions.q: Madhubala worked early in her career at the Bombay studio whose actress-cofounder is widely credited with giving her the screen name 'Madhubala'. Which studio was this?
questions.explanation: Madhubala worked at Bombay Talkies as a young actress, and its co-founder Devika Rani is widely credited with giving her the screen name Madhubala.

questions.q: Mughal-e-Azam was originally released in 1960 mostly in black-and-white, but one major sequence was filmed in colour. Which one?
options: The 'Jab Pyar Kiya To Darna Kya' song
questions.explanation: The 'Jab Pyar Kiya To Darna Kya' number in the Sheesh Mahal was shot in colour while most of the 1960 film was black-and-white.

questions.q: In the 1949 film Mahal, opposite which leading man did Madhubala appear?
questions.explanation: Madhubala starred opposite Ashok Kumar in Kamal Amrohi's 1949 mystery Mahal.

questions.q: Which Madhubala film led to a celebrated court case after the actress and her father refused to travel for an outdoor shoot, causing a rift with co-star Dilip Kumar?
questions.explanation: A dispute over outdoor shooting for Naya Daur (1957) led to a famous court case and Madhubala being replaced by Vyjayanthimala.

questions.q: Which 1960 musical, directed by P. L. Santoshi and famous for its qawwalis, paired Madhubala with Bharat Bhushan?
questions.explanation: Madhubala starred with Bharat Bhushan in the 1960 musical Barsaat Ki Raat, known for its qawwalis composed by Roshan.

questions.q: Madhubala's film Jwala was released in 1971, after her death. In what manner did this happen?
options: It was released posthumously, having been stalled for years
It was reshot entirely with another actress
It was abandoned and never released
It was released only as a documentary
questions.explanation: Jwala, long delayed during her illness, was released in 1971, two years after Madhubala's death in 1969.

questions.q: The music for Mughal-e-Azam, including the songs picturised on Madhubala, was composed by which music director?
questions.explanation: Naushad composed the celebrated score of Mughal-e-Azam (1960).

questions.q: Decades after its 1960 release, a fully colourised version of Mughal-e-Azam was theatrically re-released in which year?
questions.explanation: A digitally colourised version of Mughal-e-Azam was re-released in 2004, introducing the film to new audiences.

questions.q: In which year did India Post issue a commemorative postage stamp honouring the actress Madhubala?
questions.explanation: India Post issued a commemorative postage stamp featuring Madhubala on 18 March 2008.
